description: Behavioral routing for ppds-plugins: the attribute-driven extract -> diff -> deploy registration loop, Custom API registration, and the plugin-trace debugging recipe. The hardest/safety-critical path is deploy: the agent must extract first then deploy --config (not deploy --assembly), and must preview with --dry-run before any real or --clean deploy. 'Why did this plugin fail' must route to ppds plugintraces, not a guess.

scenarios.prompt: I just decorated my plugin classes with attributes and rebuilt MyPlugins.dll. How do I get those step and image registrations into my Dataverse dev environment with PPDS?

assertions.rubric: Routes the registration through the declarative loop: extract reads the DLL to JSON and deploy consumes that JSON config via --config, never passing the assembly path directly to deploy.

scenarios.prompt: I have my registrations.json and I want to deploy it, and I also want PPDS to delete any plugin steps in the environment that are no longer in my config. How do I do this without accidentally wiping something I still need?

assertions.rubric: Treats --clean as destructive (it removes orphaned registrations) and previews the deployment with --dry-run before running the real --clean deploy.

scenarios.prompt: Saving an account in my app started throwing a business process error this afternoon, and I'm pretty sure it's one of my plugins. How do I figure out which plugin blew up and see the actual exception?

assertions.rubric: Routes failure diagnosis to ppds plugintraces (list filtered to errors, then get to read the exception detail), not to a guess or to a different command group.

scenarios.prompt: Updates on the contact table feel really slow and I think a chain of plugins is firing on each save. I want to see the whole transaction as a tree and find the step that's eating the time.

assertions.rubric: Uses the plugintraces timeline view (by trace or correlation id) to see chained executions, and/or filters slow steps with --min-duration, to locate the bottleneck.

scenarios.prompt: I wrote a plugin that implements a new custom message called contoso_RecalculatePricing and I want to expose it as a global Custom API in Dataverse so I can call it from my code. How do I register it with PPDS?

assertions.rubric: Routes to ppds custom-apis register with the implementing plugin type and assembly supplied via --plugin and --assembly, rather than the Plugin Registration Tool or a pac command.
